ogTitle: Rawsonville vs Raymond Terrace: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Rawsonville and Raymond Terrace.
heading: Rawsonville vs Raymond Terrace — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Rawsonville, New South Wales and Raymond Terrace, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Rawsonville has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Raymond Terrace (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Rawsonville or Raymond Terrace safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Rawsonville has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Raymond Terrace scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Rawsonville scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Raymond Terrace scores 62/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Rawsonville or Raymond Terrace?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Rawsonville has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.93% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
